practice_boundaries.detail: Explicit prohibition on turning analysis into clinical decision support

methodological_ground.detail: Six overclaim prohibitions (association ≠ causation, expression validation ≠ functional proof, etc.) are methodologically rigorous

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 1 literature analysis only

reliability.note: Minimum Viable Input escalation path is excellent; one gap: plugin system may activate prematurely without clear user request in some edge cases

performance_context.note: 235 lines with 6 reference modules plus rich plugin system; progressive disclosure well-executed

human_usability.note: Description is exceptionally rich with specific trigger examples; excellent discoverability across all user types

agent_specific.note: Four output modes, four tracks, five hybrid sub-tracks, and plugin system provide excellent composability and scope coverage

inputs.label: Standard analysis of an RCT with full paper provided

assertions.text: Paper correctly classified by actual study design (not author self-description)
Track A deep analysis applied with all 16 checklist items
What the paper can claim vs cannot claim sections both present and distinct
Evidence strength rating given with rationale tied to design features
Section 14 follow-up questions tailored to paper-specific findings

inputs.label: TCGA + qPCR + cell assay hybrid paper — Track D1

assertions.text: Hybrid mode activated with primary (Track B) and secondary (Track C) tracks assigned
Evidence hierarchy summary present for multi-track paper
SHAP/feature importance not used as proxy for biological proof
Expression validation not presented as functional proof
Bioinformatics findings not presented as therapeutic target confirmation

inputs.label: Bioinformatics paper with NHANES + ML — Track D2

assertions.text: Track D2 activated with 10 ML-clinical checklist items applied
Internal validation not presented as clinical deployment readiness
Public database significance not equated with therapeutic target confirmation
ML model limitations explicitly identified
Association-causation distinction maintained throughout

inputs.label: Only DOI provided — paper not retrievable directly

assertions.text: Minimum Viable Input escalation path applied: states what cannot be done with DOI alone
User asked to provide abstract or methods text to proceed
No paper content fabricated from DOI alone
Any inferred analysis from title/journal labeled as preliminary
Response practically useful despite input limitation

inputs.label: Paper with overclaiming language — 'proved causation', 'ready for clinical translation'

assertions.text: Overclaiming language explicitly flagged in Section 8 (What the Paper Cannot Claim)
Causal language from paper correctly classified as overclaim if not supported
Clinical translation readiness claim addressed with correct evidence boundary
Balanced critical appraisal maintained despite paper's confident language
Biased analysis request ('positive only') handled with explanation then full report

inputs.label: Request to write manuscript Introduction from scratch — out of scope

assertions.text: Writing request correctly identified as outside skill scope
Redirect to academic writing skill provided
No manuscript section generated before scope check
Response concise and actionable
Any analysis-relevant output limited to reading the provided paper

inputs.label: Request for 'quick summary only, no weaknesses' — biased analysis request

assertions.text: Biased analysis request addressed with brief explanation of balanced design
Full balanced report produced despite strength-only request
Weaknesses not omitted from critical appraisal
Quick Read mode offered as alternative for legitimate speed-reading need
Explanation of why balanced appraisal matters provided before full report
assertions.note: Explanation was present but very brief (one sentence) — could be more persuasive about why this matters for manuscript integrity

key_strengths: Four output modes (Quick Read/Standard/Expert Deep Review/Output-Targeted) with clear activation logic provide exceptional flexibility for different use cases
Five evidence tracks including three hybrid tracks cover the full range of modern biomedical literature types
Six explicit overclaim prohibitions are among the most comprehensive anti-inflation safeguards in this entire skill collection
Minimum Viable Input escalation path ensures graceful handling of any input type from full PDF to title-only

recommendations.title: Balanced-appraisal explanation too brief when user requests biased analysis
recommendations.problem: When a user requests a strength-only analysis, the explanation of why balanced appraisal is necessary is too brief (one sentence) to be persuasive for users unfamiliar with evidence appraisal principles.
recommendations.root_cause: Behavioral rule says 'briefly explain' which is correctly interpreted as minimal explanation.
recommendations.fix: Expand the biased-request response to 2-3 sentences explaining: (1) what balanced appraisal prevents (manuscript credibility risk, reviewer criticism), (2) that Quick Read mode is available for faster reading, and (3) that weaknesses are noted alongside strengths — not as criticism but as scope-setting.

recommendations.title: Plugin activation boundary slightly unclear for Output-Targeted Mode
recommendations.problem: The skill says 'offer, do not auto-activate plugins' but does not define when it is appropriate to proactively suggest a specific plugin vs offering all plugins equally.
recommendations.root_cause: Plugin activation guidance is defined in references/plugins.md which is not loaded unless referenced.
recommendations.fix: Add a brief plugin suggestion heuristic to SKILL.md: 'After Standard report, offer the 1-2 most relevant plugins based on paper type — for bioinformatics papers offer Replication Starter; for mechanism papers offer Follow-Up Experiment Designer; for clinical papers offer PI Decision Brief.'
